_meta.description: Neutral policy position taxonomy. Approved entries are used by scrape_candidate_positions.py to map candidate stances to consistent, plain-English statements. Each position carries its own source URL and the raw text it was derived from for individual QA verification.
framing_rules: Primary: 'is for X' — when the candidate has a stated positive position
Fallback: 'does not support X' — only when no positive alternative is stated on their site
Never use 'opposes' — adversarial framing, reads like an attack
6th grade reading level, Hemingway word economy — one concrete sentence per position
Describe the actual policy proposal, not the political tribe or the opponent
If a candidate is against amnesty but states no alternative, write 'does not support amnesty' not 'supports deportation'

position_format.statement: The neutral 'is for X' or 'does not support X' sentence
position_format.source: URL of the specific page this position was found on — required for QA
position_format.derived_from: The raw text from that page the statement was synthesized from — audit trail

_meta.notes: Healthcare is pre-seeded as a format reference with placeholder sources. Run 'python build_policy_taxonomy.py --issue Healthcare --refresh' to replace placeholders with real sourced entries. All other categories start as empty arrays — run build_policy_taxonomy.py to populate them one at a time.

Healthcare.statement: is for Medicare for All — one government-run plan replacing private insurance
is for adding a public option alongside private insurance
is for keeping the current system with targeted reforms to lower costs
is for removing mandates and expanding private market competition
is for repealing the ACA and returning health decisions to states
